Borrower must have greater than $250 million in tangible assets to which the ADI has unconditional recourse for the 'not dependent' classification to apply.

ADI must apply a 150 per cent risk weight to all ADC exposures that do not qualify for the 100 per cent treatment.

condition_context: {"exposure_type": "non-residential defaulted", "provision_coverage": ">=20 percent of outstanding exposure amount"}
consequence: Risk weight of 100 per cent applies where provisions are at least 20 per cent of the outstanding defaulted exposure amount.

condition_context: {"exposure_type": "non-residential defaulted", "provision_coverage": "<20 percent of outstanding exposure amount"}
consequence: Risk weight of 150 per cent applies where provisions are less than 20 per cent of the outstanding defaulted exposure amount.

condition_context: {"exposure_type": "non-residential defaulted", "metric_description": "provisions as percentage of outstanding exposure amount"}
consequence: If provisions are at or above 20 per cent of outstanding exposure, a 100 per cent risk weight applies; below 20 per cent, a 150 per cent risk weight applies.
Received collateral must be marked-to-market and revalued at least every six months to be recognised under the simple approach.

condition_context: {"approach": "simple approach", "portion": "collateral-secured portion of exposure", "exceptions": "paragraphs 18 to 20 of Attachment G"}
consequence: The risk weight on the collateral-secured portion of an exposure under the simple approach must not fall below 20 per cent, except under specified conditions.
A zero per cent risk weight may be applied where the exposure and cash collateral are denominated in the same currency.

condition_context: {"approach": "simple approach", "collateral_type": "sovereign securities eligible for zero per cent risk weight", "currency_match": true}
consequence: Where sovereign securities are used as collateral for a zero per cent risk weight transaction, the market value must be discounted by 20 per cent.

condition_context: {"approach": "simple approach", "transaction_type": "SFT", "conditions_met": "paragraphs 19(a)-(h) of Attachment G", "counterparty": "non-core market participant"}
consequence: Qualifying SFTs may be risk-weighted at 10 per cent where the counterparty is not a core market participant.

condition_context: {"approach": "simple approach", "transaction_type": "SFT", "conditions_met": "paragraphs 19(a)-(h) of Attachment G", "counterparty": "core market participant as defined in paragraph 20"}
consequence: Qualifying SFTs may be risk-weighted at zero per cent where the counterparty is a core market participant.

condition_context: {"transaction_type": "SFT", "event": "counterparty failure to remargin", "measurement": "time between last mark-to-market before failure to remargin and liquidation of collateral"}
consequence: For a qualifying SFT, the time between the last mark-to-market before failure to remargin and liquidation of collateral must be no more than four business days.

condition_context: {"approach": "simple approach", "collateral_type": "long-term debt securities issued by sovereigns", "rating_source": "ECAI"}
consequence: Long-term sovereign debt securities are eligible collateral under the simple approach only if they have a credit rating grade of four or better.

condition_context: {"approach": "simple approach", "collateral_type": "short-term or long-term debt securities issued by non-sovereign counterparties", "rating_source": "ECAI"}
consequence: Short-term or long-term debt securities issued by non-sovereign counterparties are eligible collateral under the simple approach only if they have a credit rating grade of three or better.

condition_context: {"approach": "simple approach", "collateral_type": "unrated bank senior debt listed on recognised exchange", "condition": "all rated issues of same seniority by issuing bank have long-term or short-term credit rating grade of at least three"}
consequence: Unrated bank senior debt is eligible collateral under the simple approach only if all rated issues of the same seniority by the issuing bank have a credit rating grade of at least three.

condition_context: {"approach": "simple approach", "collateral_type": "senior securitisation exposures as defined in APS 120"}
consequence: Senior securitisation exposures are eligible collateral under the simple approach only if they have an external credit rating grade of one.
Units in unlisted trusts are eligible collateral under the simple approach only if the ADI can redeem its investment within three business days.

condition_context: {"condition": "maturity_mismatch_exists", "description": "Collateral recognition where residual maturity of collateral is less than maturity of exposure"}
consequence: Collateral may only be recognised if original maturity of term of lodgement is at least 12 months

condition_context: {"condition": "maturity_mismatch_exists", "description": "Collateral recognition where residual maturity of collateral is less than maturity of exposure"}
consequence: Collateral may only be recognised if residual maturity is at least 3 months
